question.label: Broad reviews gap
question.name: When feedback is broad, what part of your work gets missed most often?
question.description: Turns his complaint about broad reviews into a concrete recurring gap in the work.

answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.

assessment.note: He pivoted from the asked work example to a growth need, signaling strong lack of clarity about what to change while not giving direct wellbeing signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] concept rationale growth thread surfaced but drill cap forces arc advance.

new_queue.label: Missed signal
new_queue.name: Where do you think your work most often misses the mark?

new_queue.label: Growth blocker
new_queue.name: What is the main thing that is slowing your growth right now?

new_queue.label: Support needed
new_queue.name: What would help you make that practice stick this month?

history.answer_excerpt: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.

I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
